title: Repo-agnostic engine: run the harness against any target repo
description: Every module hardcodes REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent, so the harness only operates on its own repo. Make the engine resolve a target repo from ADW_REPO (else the git top-level of cwd, else the engine repo for self-hosting). Per-project mutable state (prd.json, state.json, run logs, .claude/settings.json) lives in the target repo; prompt/command assets and configs/ default to the engine but a target-side .adw/ override wins if present. Package the engine so it is installable and invokable from any repo. This is the unlock for using the harness to build other projects.
acceptance_criteria: A single resolver (e.g. adw/paths.py) returns the target root from ADW_REPO, else `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, else the engine repo; all modules use it instead of the hardcoded REPO_ROOT
prd.json, state.json, and run logs are read/written in the target repo; commands/ and configs/ resolve from the engine unless a target .adw/commands or .adw/configs exists, which takes precedence
Running ADW_REPO=/path/to/project against a workflow operates entirely on that project (branch, commits, logs land there) and leaves the engine repo untouched
With ADW_REPO unset and cwd in the engine repo, behavior is unchanged (self-hosting preserved)
A documented .adw/ skeleton (prd.json, configs, .claude/settings.json) turns a fresh repo into a target with no code copied
Unit tests cover path resolution (env override, git-toplevel fallback, per-project config override) against a temp repo; full suite uv run pytest -q stays green
